title: Pilosella pseudothaumasia
intro: Pilosella pseudothaumasia, also known as a type of dandelion, is a perennial plant species in the Asteraceae family, characterized by its yellow flowers and jagged leaves. This plant has been traditionally used for its purported health benefits, including treating digestive issues, such as constipation and ulcers, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Pilosella pseudothaumasia include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support liver and digestive health.
taxonomy: Pilosella pseudothaumasia is classified within the family Asteraceae, and its full classification is as follows: Pilosella pseudothaumasia belongs to the kingdom Plantae, class Magnoliopsida, order Asterales, family Asteraceae, genus Pilosella, and species P. pseudothaumasia. This classification places Pilosella pseudothaumasia within the group of flowering plants. Pilosella pseudothaumasia's classification as a member of the Asteraceae family, commonly known as the daisy family, highlights its relationship to other flowering plants in this group. The species Pilosella pseudothaumasia can be further distinguished from other Pilosella species through its unique characteristics and classification. Its classification reflects the complex evolutionary history and relationships among flowering plants.
